rulings.Case Title: CHERYL WOODIE VS. CITY COLLEGE OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Duplicate entry. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CHERYL WOODIE VS. CITY COLLEGE OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied. Triable issue of fact raised regarding estoppel. See Elmore vs. Oak Valley Hospital District (1988) 204 Cal.App.3d 716. Triable issue of fact raised whether inadequately lit stairway and ending of the handrail created a dangerous condition. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT AMANDA HEIER, VALERIE GOLD, and, MARY ELIZABETH INN, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Off Calendar, Defendants dismissed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CORY OBERDORFER VS. TURN("THE UTILITY REFORM NETWORK"), A NON PROFIT B et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2009, line 3, PLAINTIFF CORY OBERDORFER MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion denied. Plaintiff fails to maintain his summary adjudication burden. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate the amount of his damages. To grant summary adjudication, plaintiff needs to present evidence regarding each element of his cause of action (including damages) and completely dispose of the cause of action. See CCP section 437c(f)(1) and (p)(1). Plaintiff fails to shift the burden on issues ten and eleven because he does not show he complied with defendant's grievance process. Lastly, plaintiff's motion is procedurally defective because the separate statement does not comply with the segregation requirement of CRC 3.1350(b) and (h). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA TRAN VS. BAYVIEW CAPITAL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Expunge Lis Pendens, Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT RACHEL GISHKIN, KAREN SARID, SHELLA CERVANTES, SHIRLEY CERVANTES, MARK CERVANTES, CHI-LEUNG CHENG, ALICE CHANG, GUJRAL FAMILY FOUNDATION, and, THEODORE GRADMAN Motion to Expunge Lis Pendens. Motion to expunge lis pendens granted. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA TRAN VS. BAYVIEW CAPITAL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT RACHEL GISHKIN, KAREN SARID, SHELLA CERVANTES, MARK CERVANTES, CHI-LEUNG CHENG, ALICE CHANG, GUJRAL FAMILY FOUNDATION, and, THEODORE GRADMAN DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first, fifth, seventh, and eighth causes of action. Plaintiff to plead specific facts that support each cause of action. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to second, tenth, and eleventh causes of action. Usary not applicable because licensed real estate broker arranged for the loan, plaintiff is not a "consumer" and the loan is not a "good" or "service". Injunction is a remedy. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the ninth cause of action to allege notice and restoration under Civil Code section 1691. Demurrer overruled as to the twelfth cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA TRAN VS. BAYVIEW CAPITAL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT MARIANN FINEGOLD DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first, seventh, and eighth causes of action. Plaintiff to plead specific facts to support each cause of action. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action. Defendant owed no duty to plaintiff. Demurerr overruled as to the twelfth cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA TRAN VS. BAYVIEW CAPITAL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT MARIANN FINEGOLD MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff to allege how she is entitled to attorney fees. Plaintiff to allege facts against this defendant that support oppression, fraud, or malice. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SM CONSTRUCTION & ASSOCIATES, INC VS. BACE DEVELOPMENT, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK N.A. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Civil Code section 3264 bars this claim. Plaintiffs have not alleged the existence of a direct written contract. No reasonable probability that the defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR BARKHORDARIAN VS. JASON REZAIAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT JASON REZAIAN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off Calendar. Motion and demurrer were filed on September 18, 2009 and defendant does not provide sufficient notice under CCP 1005. Defendant did not obtain an Order Stortening Time and there is no special Unlawful Detainer Statute governing the calendaring of demurrers and motions to strike. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR BARKHORDARIAN VS. JASON REZAIAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 25, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT JASON REZAIAN MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion and demurrer were filed on September 18, 2009 and defendant does not provide sufficient notice under CCP 1005. Defendant did not obtain an Order Shortening Time and there is no special Unlawful Detainer Statute governing the calendaring of demurrers and motions to strike. =(302/CWW)
